content.full_text:
October 27, 1976


Hon. Harold C. Brown
Court of Appeals
First Appellate District
4154 State Building
San Francisco, CA  94102

Dear Mr. Brown:

Your letter of October 12, 1976, addressed to
Secretary of State March Fong Eu, requesting advice
regarding the disclosure of financial interests has
been referred to this office for response.

Please be advised that as a retiring Justice of
the Court of Appeals, you are required to file a
Statement of Economic Interests within 30 days after
leaving office. Enclosed for this purpose is a State-
ment of Economic Interests, which you should file
with the clerk of the court, who will make and retain
a copy and forward the original to this office.

If you have any questions, please do not hesitate
to contact this office.

Sincerely,

[illegible]

Dawn Wiser
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

[This document appears to be printed in reverse/mirror image, making it illegible in standard reading. The text is upside down and backwards throughout the page. Without being able to properly orient and read the reversed text, I cannot provide an accurate transcription while following the rule to transcribe exactly what is visible.]

STATE OF CALIFORNIA

Court of Appeal                                                      FPPC

FIRST DISTRICT—DIVISION THREE
STATE BUILDING, SAN FRANCISCO                          Oct 20  3 53 PM '76

HAROLD C. BROWN                                    October 12, 1976
ASSOCIATE JUSTICE


Hon. March Fong Eu
Secretary of State
925 L Street
Sacramento, California  95814

Dear Mrs. Eu:

          I am retiring as a Justice of the Court

of Appeal on October 31, 1976. I would appreciate

it if you would advise me if it is necessary that

disclosure be made of my financial interests.

                                    Very truly yours,

                                    Harold C. Brown

HCB:hd

[illegible]

[illegible] [illegible]

[illegible] [illegible]

[The majority of the document content is too faint/illegible to transcribe accurately]

[STAMP: Circular library stamp with text reading "SECRETARIAT OF [illegible]" and what appears to be a date "4 [illegible] 19[illegible]"]

Brown Dear Mr. Brown: Your letter of October 15, 1956, requesting to file office for lease/base. Regarding the disposition of financial interests per statement of Title Mary Loue Bn, lease/base applies Please be advised that as a refunding notice of your lettering of your office for lease/base. As body and forming the original to your office. With the duty of the court, who will make any revision least/ban office. The main stamp reads: "RECEIVED OCT 141976 A" and "SECRETARY OF STATE".
